machine.name: PatientWellness
machine.description: Holistic wellness monitoring for assisted living residents across six dimensions: mental acuity, anxiety, stress, socialization, nutrition, and exercise. Produces a four-level wellness metric (OPTIMAL / GOOD / ALERT / CRITICAL) routed into the perceptual resolution space for longitudinal tracking and intervention planning. Sequences are calibrated to age-group norms for the 65-74, 75-84, and 85+ cohorts.

metadata.author: Reality Engine
metadata.eventSpace: 8D binary wellness space: [MENTAL_ACUITY, ANXIETY_OK, STRESS_OK, SOCIAL_OK, NUTRITION_OK, EXERCISE_OK, DECLINE_TREND, AGE_ADJUSTED]
metadata.outputSpace: 8D four-level wellness metric: [OPTIMAL, GOOD, ALERT, CRITICAL, _, _, _, _]

cohort_65_74.MENTAL_ACUITY: MMSE ≥27 or MoCA ≥26; orientation, recall, executive function within published age norms
cohort_65_74.ANXIETY_OK: GAD-7 score ≤7; no significant anxiety interfering with daily function
cohort_65_74.STRESS_OK: Perceived Stress Scale ≤13; cortisol/sleep quality indicators within range
cohort_65_74.SOCIAL_OK: ≥3 meaningful social interactions per week (family, friends, group activities)
cohort_65_74.NUTRITION_OK: ≥1600 kcal/day with adequate protein (≥0.8g/kg); MNA screening ≥24
cohort_65_74.EXERCISE_OK: ≥150 min/week moderate-intensity activity, or ≥75 min vigorous; strength 2×/week

cohort_75_84.MENTAL_ACUITY: MMSE ≥24 or MoCA ≥22; adjusted for mild age-related processing speed decline
cohort_75_84.ANXIETY_OK: GAD-7 score ≤10; some worry acceptable if not impairing function
cohort_75_84.STRESS_OK: Perceived Stress Scale ≤16; accounting for increased life-event stress load
cohort_75_84.SOCIAL_OK: ≥2 meaningful social interactions per week; telephone/video contacts count
cohort_75_84.NUTRITION_OK: ≥1400 kcal/day with adequate protein (≥1.0g/kg for sarcopenia prevention); MNA ≥23
cohort_75_84.EXERCISE_OK: ≥150 min/week any intensity (walking counts); balance training encouraged; D7=L when met

cohort_85_plus.MENTAL_ACUITY: MMSE ≥18 or MoCA ≥17; adjusted for oldest-old norms; decline rate more important than absolute score
cohort_85_plus.ANXIETY_OK: GAD-7 score ≤12; focus on clinically impairing anxiety only
cohort_85_plus.STRESS_OK: Subjective wellbeing and affect balance; caregiver-observed distress scale ≤3
cohort_85_plus.SOCIAL_OK: ≥1 meaningful social interaction per week; passive engagement (communal dining) counts
cohort_85_plus.NUTRITION_OK: ≥1200 kcal/day; adequate hydration; MNA ≥22; supplement use acceptable
cohort_85_plus.EXERCISE_OK: Any structured movement: chair exercises, stretching, supervised walks. Sedentary (no structured activity) → D5=L, D7=H (AGE_ADJUSTED flag). Walks to dining room and activities = D5=H for this cohort.

dimensions.D0_MENTAL_ACUITY: Cognitive assessment (MMSE/MoCA/ADL) is within the resident's age-cohort norm. Includes orientation, recall, executive function, and language comprehension.
dimensions.D1_ANXIETY_OK: Anxiety screening (GAD-7 or equivalent) within age-adjusted acceptable range. HIGH means anxiety is managed and not clinically impairing.
dimensions.D2_STRESS_OK: Physiological and psychological stress indicators (PSS, sleep quality, affect balance) within age-adjusted normal range. HIGH means stress is controlled.
dimensions.D3_SOCIAL_OK: Social engagement frequency meets age-cohort guideline. Includes group activities, family visits, one-on-one peer interaction, and video/telephone contact.
dimensions.D4_NUTRITION_OK: Dietary intake meets age-adjusted caloric, protein, and hydration targets. MNA screening score above age-specific threshold.
dimensions.D5_EXERCISE_OK: Physical activity meets age-adjusted guideline. HIGH when structured movement meets cohort norm (150 min/week moderate for 65-74; any structured activity for 85+).
dimensions.D6_DECLINE_TREND: Longitudinal tracking flag — set by care team when ≥2 wellness dimensions have shown a worsening trend across consecutive assessment cycles (typically 2-week intervals).
dimensions.D7_AGE_ADJUSTED: Age-adjusted assessment flag — set when the exercise (D5) norm has been calibrated to a reduced standard for the resident's age cohort (typically 75+ or when standard exercise norms cannot be safely met). All other five wellness dimensions are still fully evaluated.

vectorEncoding.semantics: GTE binary: value>=threshold → HIGH (within norm / positive indicator), value<threshold → LOW (below norm / concern). Sequences accumulate HIGH wellness dimensions one at a time (HD=1 per step) to represent the progressive confirmation of each wellness domain during a care review cycle. D6 and D7 are contextual modifier flags, not wellness assessment dimensions.

wellnessLevels.OPTIMAL: Level 4 — All six wellness dimensions within age-adjusted norms. No decline trend. Resident is thriving; maintain current care plan.
wellnessLevels.GOOD: Level 3 — Five of six dimensions within norms, or 5/5 non-exercise dimensions within norms for residents where exercise is age-adjusted (D7=H). Minor gap does not require clinical intervention; note and monitor.
wellnessLevels.ALERT: Level 2 — Three of six dimensions within norms, with an active decline trend (D6=H). Multiple deficits and worsening trajectory require care plan review and targeted interventions.
wellnessLevels.CRITICAL: Level 1 — Mental acuity has fallen below age-cohort norm with an established decline trend (D6=H). Multi-system wellness failure; clinical evaluation and immediate care plan revision required.

singleTransitionAssumption.notes: All within-sequence transitions change exactly one binary dimension (HD=1). Sequences 1-3 and 5 accumulate wellness dimensions one per step. Sequence 4 (critical) steps from decline-with-mental-borderline (D0=H,D6=H) to mental-failing (D0=L,D6=H) with HD=1. Sequence 5 parallels Sequence 1 with D7=H constant throughout, shifting to the age-adjusted perceptual lane.

metadata.regex: OPTIMAL: (MENTAL)+ · ANXIETY · STRESS · SOCIAL · NUTRITION · EXERCISE → OPTIMAL  |  GOOD: (MENTAL)+ · ANXIETY · SOCIAL_NO_STRESS · NUTRITION · EXERCISE → GOOD  |  ALERT: (MENTAL)+ · SOCIAL_NO_ANX · NUTRITION · DECLINE → ALERT  |  CRITICAL: (DECLINING_MENTAL)+ · ACUITY_FAIL → CRITICAL  |  OPTIMAL_ADJ: (MENTAL_ADJ)+ · ANXIETY_ADJ · STRESS_ADJ · SOCIAL_ADJ · NUTRITION_ADJ → GOOD[age-adjusted]

governance.notes: Auto-backfilled by scripts/backfill-governance.mjs from primaryDomain="health-personal".  Review and tune the routing as the team owning PatientWellness matures.

rules.description: Level 4 — OPTIMAL: All six wellness dimensions within age-adjusted norms. Resident is thriving. Maintain current care plan.
Level 3 — GOOD: Five wellness dimensions in norm; stress elevated but not clinically impairing. Monitor and offer stress-reduction activities.
Level 2 — ALERT: Multiple wellness deficits (anxiety, stress, exercise) with active decline trend. Care plan review and targeted interventions required.
Level 1 — CRITICAL: Mental acuity below age-cohort norm with established decline trend. Immediate clinical evaluation and care plan revision required.
Level 3 — GOOD (Age-Adjusted): Five wellness dimensions in norm; exercise below standard guideline but within age-adjusted functional capacity. Not penalized against an inapplicable standard.

sequences.name: Optimal Wellness — All Six Dimensions in Norm

metadata.description: All six wellness dimensions confirmed within age-adjusted norms. No decline trend. Applies to all age cohorts when the assessor calibrates each dimension to the resident's age group before scoring.
metadata.regex: (MENTAL_NORM)+ · ANXIETY_OK · STRESS_OK · SOCIAL_OK · NUTRITION_OK · EXERCISE_OK → OPTIMAL
metadata.path: MENTAL_NORM → ANXIETY_OK → STRESS_OK → SOCIAL_OK → NUTRITION_OK → EXERCISE_OK
metadata.cohortApplicability: 65-74: standard norms for all dimensions. 75-84: exercise at moderate intensity (30+ min walks). 85+: chair yoga, supervised walks, or any structured movement qualifies as D5=H.

metadata.name: Mental Acuity In Norm
metadata.description: First wellness domain confirmed: cognitive assessment scores within age-cohort norm. Kleene-plus allows multiple repeated cognitive observations (e.g., weekly MMSE screenings) before the full care review cycle advances.

metadata.name: Anxiety Within Norm
metadata.description: Anxiety screening within age-adjusted acceptable range. GAD-7 or equivalent confirms no clinically significant anxiety. HD=1 from MENTAL_NORM (D1_ANXIETY_OK: L→H).

metadata.name: Stress Within Norm
metadata.description: Stress indicators (Perceived Stress Scale, sleep quality, affect balance) within age-adjusted range. HD=1 from ANXIETY_OK (D2_STRESS_OK: L→H).

metadata.name: Socialization Within Norm
metadata.description: Social engagement frequency meets age-cohort guideline. Confirmed via activity log, family visit records, and staff observation. HD=1 from STRESS_OK (D3_SOCIAL_OK: L→H).

metadata.name: Nutrition Within Norm
metadata.description: Nutritional intake meets age-adjusted caloric and protein targets. MNA screening above age-specific threshold. HD=1 from SOCIAL_OK (D4_NUTRITION_OK: L→H).

metadata.name: Exercise Within Norm — All Dimensions Confirmed
metadata.description: Physical activity meets age-adjusted guideline. All six wellness dimensions now confirmed in norm. Terminal node — fires OPTIMAL output. HD=1 from NUTRITION_OK (D5_EXERCISE_OK: L→H).
Level 4 — OPTIMAL: All six wellness dimensions within age-adjusted norms. Resident is thriving. Maintain current care plan.
metadata.action: Continue current care plan. Document as reference baseline for future assessments.

sequences.name: Good Wellness — Stress Sub-Optimal (Five Dimensions In Norm)

metadata.description: Five of six wellness dimensions confirmed in norm. Stress (D2) is below target — common in residents managing life transitions, bereavement, or care-adjustment stress. Mental acuity, anxiety, socialization, nutrition, and exercise are all adequate. No decline trend.
metadata.stressGapContext: Stress elevated but anxiety managed — a common dissociation in early adjustment periods. D2_STRESS_OK=L but D1_ANXIETY_OK=H indicates the resident is emotionally regulated despite elevated stress load.

metadata.name: Mental Acuity In Norm
metadata.description: Cognitive assessment within norm. Same initial as Sequence 1 — both sequences activate on MENTAL_NORM events and diverge at the third assessment step when stress (D2) is confirmed absent.

metadata.name: Anxiety Managed
metadata.description: Anxiety within acceptable range. D2_STRESS_OK remains L — stress is elevated but does not yet cross into anxiety disorder territory. HD=1 from MENTAL_NORM (D1: L→H).

metadata.name: Socialization OK (Stress Still Elevated)
metadata.description: Social engagement meets norm. D2_STRESS_OK remains L (stress not managed). Resident is socially connected despite life stress — a protective factor. HD=1 from ANXIETY_OK (D3: L→H; D2 stays L distinguishing this path from Sequence 1's STRESS_OK step).

metadata.name: Nutrition OK
metadata.description: Nutritional intake meets norm. Four dimensions confirmed (mental, anxiety, social, nutrition). Stress remains below norm. HD=1 from SOCIAL_NO_STRESS (D4: L→H).

metadata.name: Exercise OK — Good Wellness Confirmed
metadata.description: Physical activity meets norm. Five of six wellness dimensions confirmed. Stress remains the only gap. No decline trend. Terminal node — fires GOOD output. HD=1 from NUTRITION_OK (D5: L→H).
Level 3 — GOOD: Five wellness dimensions in norm; stress elevated but not clinically impairing. Monitor and offer stress-reduction activities.
metadata.action: Offer stress-reduction programming (mindfulness, pet therapy, chaplaincy). Reassess stress dimension at next cycle. No care plan change required at this time.

sequences.name: Alert Wellness — Multiple Deficits with Decline Trend

metadata.description: Three of six wellness dimensions in norm (mental acuity, socialization, nutrition). Anxiety and stress are both elevated (D1=L, D2=L), exercise is below norm (D5=L). Longitudinal decline trend detected (D6=H). Requires care plan review and targeted interventions.
metadata.clinicalContext: Anxiety and stress co-elevation is common in residents experiencing: new diagnosis burden, family caregiver stress, social isolation compounded by mobility decline, or bereavement. The D6_DECLINE_TREND flag indicates this is not a single-assessment anomaly but a worsening pattern.

metadata.name: Mental Acuity Still In Norm
metadata.description: Cognitive function holds despite other wellness deficits. Kleene-plus allows repeated mental assessments. This sequence diverges from the optimal path at the second step — anxiety (D1) is not confirmed, so the sequence advances to social (D3) directly.

metadata.name: Socialization OK (Anxiety Elevated)
metadata.description: Social engagement meets norm. Anxiety (D1) and stress (D2) remain LOW — both are below norm. Resident is connecting socially but managing elevated emotional distress. HD=1 from MENTAL_NORM (D3: L→H; D1 stays L — differs from Sequence 2 which advances D1 first).

metadata.name: Nutrition OK
metadata.description: Nutritional intake meets norm. Three dimensions confirmed (mental, social, nutrition). Anxiety, stress, and exercise all below norm. Assessment is converging toward an alert classification. HD=1 from SOCIAL_NO_ANX (D4: L→H).

metadata.name: Decline Trend Confirmed
metadata.description: Care team has flagged a longitudinal decline trend (D6_DECLINE_TREND: L→H) — multiple consecutive assessment cycles have shown worsening in ≥2 dimensions. Combined with three active deficits (anxiety, stress, exercise), this confirms an alert-level wellness state. HD=1 from NUTRITION_OK (D6: L→H).
Level 2 — ALERT: Multiple wellness deficits (anxiety, stress, exercise) with active decline trend. Care plan review and targeted interventions required.
metadata.action: Schedule multidisciplinary care plan conference within 5 business days. Refer for: anxiety/stress counseling or psychiatric consultation, physical therapy for activity re-engagement, occupational therapy for lifestyle restructuring. Increase assessment frequency to weekly. Notify attending physician and family.

sequences.name: Critical Wellness — Mental Acuity Failure with Established Decline Trend

metadata.description: Mental acuity has fallen below the resident's age-cohort norm in the context of a previously established longitudinal decline trend (D6=H set from prior cycles). The combination of cognitive decline crossing the clinical threshold and an active worsening trajectory constitutes a critical wellness event requiring immediate clinical evaluation.
metadata.clinicalContext: The initial Kleene-plus node represents the monitoring state where the resident's cognitive scores are still borderline (within norm but near the threshold) while a decline trend has been active for 2+ prior assessment cycles. The terminal fires when the score drops below the age-cohort threshold. Applies to all age cohorts including 85+ (D7 is irrelevant to this sequence — D6 is the active flag).

metadata.name: Mental Borderline with Active Decline Trend
metadata.description: Resident's cognitive score is currently above the threshold (D0_MENTAL_ACUITY=H) but within the warning zone, while D6_DECLINE_TREND=H (set by prior assessment cycles). This is the monitoring state — Kleene-plus semantics allow the sequence to persist across multiple weekly assessments in this state before the threshold is crossed.

metadata.name: Mental Acuity Below Threshold
metadata.description: Cognitive assessment score has dropped below the age-cohort clinical threshold. D0_MENTAL_ACUITY: H→L. D6_DECLINE_TREND remains H — this is not an isolated dip but part of a confirmed worsening pattern. HD=1 from DECLINING_MENTAL (D0: H→L). Terminal node — fires CRITICAL output.
Level 1 — CRITICAL: Mental acuity below age-cohort norm with established decline trend. Immediate clinical evaluation and care plan revision required.
metadata.action: Notify attending physician immediately. Order comprehensive cognitive evaluation (neuropsychological testing, imaging if not recent). Review and revise care plan within 48 hours. Assess for reversible causes: UTI, dehydration, medication side effects, depression-related pseudodementia. Increase supervision level. Notify family and document in medical record. Consult social work for advanced care planning discussion.

sequences.name: Good Wellness — Age-Adjusted (Exercise Norm Calibrated, Five Dimensions In Norm)

metadata.description: For residents where the exercise norm has been age-adjusted (D7_AGE_ADJUSTED=H) — typically 75+ where standard exercise guidelines cannot be safely met — five non-exercise dimensions confirmed in norm is classified as GOOD rather than penalizing the resident against an inapplicable standard. Applies when D5_EXERCISE_OK=L is expected and acceptable given the resident's functional capacity.

ageCohortNotes.cohort_75_84: D5=L when resident's physical capacity has declined below even moderate-intensity exercise. D7=H set by assessor. All five remaining dims confirmed → GOOD (not penalized for functional limitation).
ageCohortNotes.cohort_85_plus: D5=L when resident is fully sedentary (no structured movement program at all). Residents who walk to dining room or do chair exercises should have D5=H set (gentle activity threshold) and fire Sequence 1 (OPTIMAL) instead.

metadata.name: Mental Acuity In Norm (Age-Adjusted Cohort)
metadata.description: Cognitive assessment within age-adjusted norm. D7_AGE_ADJUSTED=H is set for this resident indicating age-calibrated norms apply. This initial vector is distinct from Sequences 1-3 (which have D7=L) — it activates only for residents in the age-adjusted cohort. Kleene-plus allows repeated observations.

metadata.name: Anxiety Within Norm (Age-Adjusted)
metadata.description: Anxiety screening within age-cohort norm. D7=H persists. HD=1 from MENTAL_ADJ (D1: L→H).

metadata.name: Stress Within Norm (Age-Adjusted)
metadata.description: Stress indicators within age-adjusted range. D7=H persists. HD=1 from ANXIETY_ADJ (D2: L→H).

metadata.name: Socialization Within Norm (Age-Adjusted)
metadata.description: Social engagement meets age-adjusted guideline. D7=H persists. HD=1 from STRESS_ADJ (D3: L→H).

metadata.name: Nutrition Within Norm — Good Wellness (Age-Adjusted)
metadata.description: Nutritional intake meets age-adjusted targets. All five evaluable wellness dimensions confirmed. Exercise (D5) is below standard norm but the age-adjustment flag (D7=H) indicates this is within acceptable functional limits for this resident. Terminal node — fires GOOD output. HD=1 from SOCIAL_ADJ (D4: L→H).
Level 3 — GOOD (Age-Adjusted): Five wellness dimensions in norm; exercise below standard guideline but within age-adjusted functional capacity. Not penalized against an inapplicable standard.
metadata.action: Document age-adjusted wellness classification. Refer to physical or occupational therapy to explore any safe exercise option (even passive range-of-motion). Continue monitoring all five assessed dimensions at standard frequency. No urgent care plan change required.

inputSequences.name: TC-01: Age 65-74 — Optimal Wellness, All Dimensions In Norm
inputSequences.pattern: MENTAL_NORM · ANXIETY_OK · STRESS_OK · SOCIAL_OK · NUTRITION_OK · EXERCISE_OK
inputSequences.description: OPTIMAL (Level 4): 69-year-old male recently relocated to assisted living from independent living. All six wellness dimensions confirmed within published 65-74 age norms. Exercise: 150+ min/week brisk walking. Social: 4 group activities/week plus daily family calls. MNA: 27. MMSE: 28. GAD-7: 3. PSS: 9.

metadata.assessmentNorms: MMSE ≥27, GAD-7 ≤7, PSS ≤13, social ≥3/week, MNA ≥24, exercise ≥150 min/week moderate

inputSequences.name: TC-02: Age 65-74 — Good Wellness, Stress Elevated (Recent Bereavement)
inputSequences.description: GOOD (Level 3): 71-year-old female, 4 months post-bereavement. Mental acuity normal (MMSE 27). Anxiety managed (GAD-7: 5). Socialization adequate (attending grief support group 2×/week plus family meals). Nutrition adequate (MNA: 24). Exercise active (water aerobics 3×/week). Stress elevated (PSS: 19) — bereavement-related but not clinically impairing. D2_STRESS_OK=L, all others H.

metadata.stressContext: Bereavement stress is time-limited and expected; anxiety management and social engagement are protective factors. Monitor PSS at next bi-weekly cycle.

inputSequences.name: TC-03: Age 65-74 — Alert Wellness, Anxiety+Stress+Exercise Deficits with Decline Trend
inputSequences.description: ALERT (Level 2): 73-year-old male with new type-2 diabetes diagnosis and recent fall. Mental acuity still within norm (MoCA: 24). Anxiety elevated (GAD-7: 14) — health anxiety about diabetes management. Stress elevated (PSS: 21) — fear of falling limits exercise participation. Social engagement maintained (D3=H). Nutrition adequate. Exercise below norm — stopped walking program after fall. Decline trend active: 3 consecutive bi-weekly assessments show worsening anxiety and decreasing activity.

metadata.deficits: D1_ANXIETY_OK=L (health anxiety), D2_STRESS_OK=L (fall fear), D5_EXERCISE_OK=L (activity cessation), D6_DECLINE_TREND=H

inputSequences.name: TC-04: Age 65-74 — Critical Wellness, Mental Decline Threshold Crossed
inputSequences.description: CRITICAL (Level 1): 72-year-old female with 6-month history of documented cognitive decline. Prior three bi-weekly assessments each showed 2-3 point MoCA drop (30→27→25→22). D6_DECLINE_TREND was set at month 4. This assessment cycle: MoCA dropped to 17, crossing below the 65-74 clinical threshold of 22. D0_MENTAL_ACUITY: H→L. Requires immediate clinical evaluation for reversible vs irreversible causes.

metadata.cognitiveTrajectory: MoCA: 30→27→25→22→17 over 6 months. D6_DECLINE_TREND set at cycle 4. CRITICAL fires at cycle 5 when D0 crosses below threshold.

inputSequences.name: TC-05: Age 75-84 — Optimal Wellness, All Dimensions In Adjusted Norm
inputSequences.pattern: MENTAL_NORM · ANXIETY_OK · STRESS_OK · SOCIAL_OK · NUTRITION_OK · EXERCISE_OK
inputSequences.description: OPTIMAL (Level 4): 79-year-old female, established resident 3 years. All six dimensions confirmed within 75-84 adjusted norms. Exercise: 30-min walks 4×/week at comfortable pace (meets 75-84 moderate-activity guideline; assessor scores D5=H). Cognitive: MMSE 25 (within 75-84 norm ≥24). Anxiety: GAD-7 8 (within adjusted 75-84 norm ≤10). Social: 3 group activities/week. Nutrition: MNA 24. PSS: 14.

metadata.assessmentNorms: MMSE ≥24, GAD-7 ≤10, PSS ≤16, social ≥2/week, MNA ≥23, any moderate activity. Note: vectors are identical to TC-01 — the machine is norm-agnostic; the assessor calibrates D values to age cohort before scoring.

inputSequences.name: TC-06: Age 75-84 — Good Wellness, Exercise Below Adjusted Norm (D7=H)
inputSequences.description: GOOD (Level 3, Age-Adjusted): 82-year-old male with osteoarthritis limiting ambulation. Mental acuity (MMSE: 24), anxiety (GAD-7: 7), stress (PSS: 12), social (2 group activities/week + daily family visits), and nutrition (MNA: 23) are all within 75-84 adjusted norms. Exercise (D5=L): unable to participate in walking program due to joint pain; no structured exercise. Assessor sets D7_AGE_ADJUSTED=H — exercise deficit is functional, not a failure of wellness engagement. Fires Sequence 5 (age-adjusted path).

metadata.ageAdjustedRationale: D7=H indicates exercise norm is calibrated to functional capacity. Resident scores GOOD (Level 3) rather than ALERT — exercise deficit is age/mobility appropriate, not a wellness failure. Refer to OT for passive range-of-motion program.

inputSequences.name: TC-07: Age 85+ — Optimal Wellness, All Age-Adjusted Dimensions In Norm
inputSequences.pattern: MENTAL_NORM · ANXIETY_OK · STRESS_OK · SOCIAL_OK · NUTRITION_OK · EXERCISE_OK
inputSequences.description: OPTIMAL (Level 4): 87-year-old female, long-term resident. Chair yoga 3×/week + walks to dining room twice daily = meets the 85+ structured movement guideline (assessor scores D5=H). MMSE: 20 (within 85+ adjusted norm ≥18). GAD-7: 9 (within 85+ adjusted norm ≤12). Social: communal dining + weekly family visit (meets 85+ norm ≥1/week). Nutrition: 1300 kcal/day with supplement (MNA: 22, within 85+ threshold). Subjective wellbeing high. D7=L (exercise norm met; age-adjusted flag not required).

metadata.assessmentNorms: MMSE ≥18, GAD-7 ≤12, caregiver-observed distress ≤3, social ≥1/week, MNA ≥22, any structured movement. D5=H because chair yoga + dining walks meet the senior movement standard.

inputSequences.name: TC-08: Age 85+ — Good Wellness, Fully Sedentary (D7=H, Exercise Age-Adjusted)
inputSequences.description: GOOD (Level 3, Age-Adjusted): 91-year-old male, bedbound due to late-stage heart failure. Cognitive intact (MMSE: 19, within 85+ norm). Anxiety managed (GAD-7: 10). Stress managed (caregiver-observed distress: 2). Social engagement via bedside visits and video calls (meets 85+ ≥1/week standard). Nutrition with supplement (MNA: 22). Exercise: fully sedentary — no structured movement possible (D5=L). Assessor sets D7=H: exercise deficit is clinical (cardiac functional limitation), not an addressable wellness gap. Five remaining dimensions all in norm.

metadata.clinicalContext: Heart failure (NYHA Class III) precludes any structured exercise. D7=H set by attending physician's wellness assessment directive. Comfort-focused care plan. Exercise deficit is medically appropriate — should not generate an ALERT or penalize wellness classification.

inputSequences.name: TC-09: Age 85+ — Critical Wellness, Cognitive Decline Threshold Crossed
inputSequences.description: CRITICAL (Level 1): 89-year-old female with progressive Alzheimer's disease. Prior three assessment cycles showed MMSE trajectory: 19→17→15 (D6_DECLINE_TREND set at cycle 2). This cycle MMSE: 13, crossing below 85+ adjusted threshold of 18. D0_MENTAL_ACUITY: H→L. Critical wellness event regardless of age cohort — cognitive function below clinical norm with confirmed longitudinal worsening trajectory. D7 is not involved in this sequence.

metadata.cognitiveTrajectory: MMSE: 19→17→15→13. D6 set at cycle 2. CRITICAL at cycle 4. Clinical evaluation for disease stage, safety assessment, advanced care planning discussion with family.

inputSequences.name: TC-10: Age 75-84 — Optimal Wellness, Multiple Repeated Cognitive Observations (Kleene+)
inputSequences.description: OPTIMAL (Level 4): 77-year-old male enrolled in a 3-week cognitive monitoring protocol. Three consecutive weekly MMSE assessments all confirm cognitive scores within norm (MMSE: 26, 26, 27) before the full bi-monthly care review advances through remaining wellness dimensions. Validates Kleene-plus MENTAL_NORM initial semantics — the sequence accumulates repeated mental assessments and does not advance until ANXIETY_OK is observed.

metadata.scenario: 3-week cognitive monitoring post-hospitalization. Three MMSE observations confirm stable cognition before full wellness review proceeds. All other dimensions confirmed at scheduled bi-monthly assessment. OPTIMAL classification issued.
